strategy.name: VWAP + RSI(3) + EMA(8) Scalping Strategy
strategy.description: Three indicators, each doing a different job. EMA(8) for trend direction, VWAP for session bias, RSI(3) for entry timing. All three must align before a trade fires.

indicators.ema_8: Trend direction. Price above EMA(8) = bullish momentum. Below = bearish.
indicators.vwap: Session bias. Price above VWAP = buyers in control. Below = sellers. Resets at midnight UTC.
indicators.rsi_3: Entry timing. RSI on 3 candles only — extremely sensitive. Below 30 in an uptrend = snap-back setup. Above 70 in a downtrend = reversal likely.

long: Price is above VWAP — buyers in control for the session
Price is above EMA(8) — uptrend confirmed
RSI(3) has dropped below 30 — short-term pullback in an uptrend, snap-back likely
short: Price is below VWAP — sellers in control
Price is below EMA(8) — downtrend confirmed
RSI(3) has spiked above 70 — overbought in a downtrend, reversal likely

exit_rules: Exit when RSI(3) crosses back above 50 (for longs) or below 50 (for shorts)
Hard stop: 0.3% below entry for longs, 0.3% above entry for shorts
Take profit at next VWAP touch or EMA(8) cross
risk_rules: Risk maximum 1% of portfolio per trade
Maximum trade size capped by MAX_TRADE_SIZE_USD in .env
Maximum trades per day capped by MAX_TRADES_PER_DAY in .env
Do not trade if price is more than 1.5% away from VWAP — overextended
Paper trade first — validate this strategy on your specific asset before going live
notes: Scalping strategy — designed for 1m to 5m timeframes. RSI(3) is intentionally fast and sensitive. Do not apply to higher timeframes.
